[291:15]off to me, feels weird. Everything feels weird lately. I don't know what that is. I think

[291:24]But tomorrow, all of a sudden is this big summit between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.

[291:31]And it feels strange because this came together very, very quickly.

[291:37]We covered this last week.

[291:40]Last week and even the week before that, Donald Trump was escalating the tension with Russia.

[291:52]towards peace and the unwillingness of Putin to negotiate.

[301:42]intending to end the war in Ukraine.

[301:55]Trump really rolled out the red carpet.

[302:19]the case that the policy of the previous administration was to completely isolate and freeze our relationship

[302:35]as much as possible from the G7 from the West, but also to the extent that it's possible

[303:02]thaw the relations, send our diplomats to meet with theirs.

[303:09]the embassies, reestablished the hotline,

[303:12]reestablished all kinds of diplomatic protocol

[303:15]between Washington and Moscow,

[303:18]which is a major concession.

[303:21]And it's a major concession because isolating Russia,

[303:25]And, putting that relationship on ice, it does hurt Russia.

[303:30]It does hurt their credibility.

[304:26]bending over backwards to accommodate Russia

[304:29]in the hopes that maybe Putin would negotiate in good faith.

[304:36]was effectively that we would simply freeze

[304:39]the conflict line.

[304:42]That wherever the Russians were,

[304:45]the extent of their advance,

[304:46]that is where the lines would be frozen,

[304:48]those would be the new borders.

[304:50]There would be a 30 day ceasefire and then that would be a springboard for a comprehensive piece.

[304:56]That was the program. That was Trump's strategy, but it didn't work. And there's a couple of reasons

[305:03]that it didn't work. In the first place, Putin does not trust the Americans at all.

[305:10]If there's a 30 day ceasefire, if they freeze the conflict lines, who does that benefit?

[305:18]And that has a lot to do with the second reason, which we'll get to, which is that Russia is winning right now.

[305:24]Russia is gaining territory at the fastest pace since the start of the war.

[305:30]And the Ukrainian line is very close to breaking. They're running out of manpower. They're having to mobilize older and younger recruits.

[305:37]They're begging for these advanced systems. The Ukrainian front line is close to collapsing.

[305:43]That is why the Russians are pushing ahead very quickly.

[305:46]They're innovating on the battlefield at a faster rate in the drone warfare domain.

[305:52]So Russia's winning the war. If they freeze the battle lines, then that gives the Americans the opportunity to resupply the Ukrainians.

[306:02]That means the Ukrainians can mobilize more manpower.

[306:05]The U.S. and NATO can mobilize more material, mobilize more equipment.

[306:12]So because Putin does not trust Trump and does not trust the West and America, why would

[306:18]he implement a 30-day ceasefire when all that would really do is break the Russians' momentum

[306:24]and give the Ukrainians a chance to consolidate?

[306:27]So he doesn't trust them.

[306:34]If Russia is pushing ahead and winning,

[306:36]freezing the battle lines means they're not gonna get

[306:39]as much as they can.

[306:42]Russia wants as much Ukrainian territory as they can get.

[306:46]And they know that the West is unwilling or unable

[306:50]to confront them or escalate their confrontation any further.

[306:54]So Putin does not wanna see a 30 day ceasefire.

[306:57]He wants a comprehensive peace.

[306:59]And he laid out his terms in Istanbul back in June.

[307:22]He says you want the fighting to stop you want to end the killing in the bloodshed you're serious about it

[307:29]He says, then we must address the fundamental causes of the conflict.

[307:34]He said, the problem is not that Russians and Ukrainians are fighting, Russians and

[308:07]Well, of course, he's referring to the expansion of NATO, that as NATO moves eastward and eventually

[308:17]It eventually poses an existential security risk to have NATO and its missiles and military

[308:24]bases so close to Russia's population and industrial core in Europe.

[308:35]That is the last country that Putin wants to see join NATO.

[308:41]Putin says to address the root causes of the conflict, here's what needs to be done.

[320:41]I just changed my mind.

[320:43]I said you have 50 days, now you have 10.

[322:14]And

[322:14]I don't know how anybody still believes this stuff anymore. I don't know who's buying it at this point

[322:20]You're gonna go and meet with Putin face-to-face to hear him out
